The biggest mistake early-stage ecom founders make is thinking they have a platform problem.

They usually don’t.

They have a product problem.

If your product can’t work on Facebook, there’s a good chance it doesn’t have real demand yet.

Because Facebook is still the cleanest

tweets.text:
Ppl are borderline psychotic now bc short forms fried their brains.

7-second dopamine hits train u to react before u think.

Instant takes. Drive-by hate.

U built a reflex on doomscrolling and now everything feels like a comment section.

Of course ur a jerk now.

Boredom = growth signal.

No longer getting dopamine from refreshing Shopify or Stripe notifications?

Congrats, u've outgrown ur current level.

Now ur brain is itching for real swings:

> Adopting a pricing change that risks lowering LTV
> Leaning into controversial UGCs ur

U don't need a perfect product.

U need customers who:
> Panic when they're running low
> Buy every upgrade u release and flex online
> Force it on everyone and their moms unprompted
> Genuinely believe their quality of life depends on it

PMF is for normies.

Build a religion

x.name: Kurt Elster

CTO. MBA. The @Shopify expert your developer follows. 
Consultant, host of The Unofficial Shopify Podcast, app dev, & agency owner. Shopify Partner since 2011.

tweets.text:
Simplest way to get better AI output: 
Use an RTF prompt.
 1. Role
2. Task
3. Format 

Give it a job title, tell it what to do, tell it how to format the output. This alone will improve everything you get from any LLM.

"Your role is copywriter, your task is to write an article,
